channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: French hantavirus patient is critically ill as outbreak grows to 11
item.description: A French woman infected in the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill and being treated with an artificial lung. The outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed.
item.content:encoded:
A French woman infected in the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill and being treated with an artificial lung. The outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed.

(Image credit: Michel Euler)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: $4.8M settlement reached over Louisiana traffic stop death, AP sources say
item.description: Louisiana officials have agreed to a tentative $4.8 million settlement with the family of Ronald Greene, a Black motorist who died during a violent roadside arrest carried out by five white officers.
item.content:encoded:
Louisiana officials have agreed to a tentative $4.8 million settlement with the family of Ronald Greene, a Black motorist who died during a violent roadside arrest carried out by five white officers.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump's proposed 'Golden Dome' estimated to cost $1.2 trillion
item.description: A Congressional Budget Office analysis suggests the cost of the missile defense program could be $1.2 trillion over the next 20 years, a far heftier sum than the initial $175 billion price tag.
item.content:encoded:
A Congressional Budget Office analysis suggests the cost of the missile defense program could be $1.2 trillion over the next 20 years, a far heftier sum than the initial $175 billion price tag.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Kids' test scores began declining way before COVID. These schools are making gains
item.description: Remember those devastating learning losses that began during the pandemic? Turns out, they began years before COVID-19. Some states are finally turning things around.
item.content:encoded: Remember those devastating learning losses that began during the pandemic? Turns out, they began years before COVID-19. Some states are finally turning things around.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Former private prison official to serve as acting ICE chief
item.description: Compared to the start of President Trump's second term, David Venturella takes over an agency with a larger workforce, more financial resources — and also a continued funding lapse.
item.content:encoded:
Compared to the start of President Trump's second term, David Venturella takes over an agency with a larger workforce, more financial resources — and also a continued funding lapse.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Jason Collins, NBA's first openly gay player, dies at 47 of brain tumor
item.description: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died after an eight-month battle with an aggressive form of a brain tumor, his family announced Tuesday.
item.content:encoded:
Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died after an eight-month battle with an aggressive form of a brain tumor, his family announced Tuesday.

(Image credit: Mark J. Terrill)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman takes the stand to fend off Elon Musk's accusations he 'stole a charity'
item.description: The trial pits two of tech's most famous personalities against each other and could lead to seismic changes for the maker of ChatGPT.
item.content:encoded:
The trial pits two of tech's most famous personalities against each other and could lead to seismic changes for the maker of ChatGPT.

(Image credit: JOSH EDELSON)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Omaha's U.S. House race tests candidates' ability to reach beyond party lines
item.description: Omaha, Neb., is home to a toss-up Congressional race this year. Voters have shown a willingness to vote for moderate candidates from both parties, but has the definition of a moderate now changed?
item.content:encoded:
Omaha, Neb., is home to a toss-up Congressional race this year. Voters have shown a willingness to vote for moderate candidates from both parties, but has the definition of a moderate now changed?

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters

item.title: About 7 million kids live in a home with a loaded and unlocked gun, a study finds
item.description: The new study in JAMA Network Open also finds that more parents leave their guns loaded and unlocked when they have teenagers, despite the fact that suicide risk goes up for this age group.
item.content:encoded:
The new study in JAMA Network Open also finds that more parents leave their guns loaded and unlocked when they have teenagers, despite the fact that suicide risk goes up for this age group.

(Image credit: Charles Krupa)
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: How Americans see China now
item.description: As President Trump heads to China this week, a new NPR-Chicago Council-Ipsos poll finds most Americans think U.S. tariffs have hurt both economies, and that the Iran war is bad for America.
item.content:encoded: As President Trump heads to China this week, a new NPR-Chicago Council-Ipsos poll finds most Americans think U.S. tariffs have hurt both economies, and that the Iran war is bad for America.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt
